camera_pose.description: Camera framing, orbit, pan, tilt, dolly, and reset controls for the theater view. Use these when you need repeatable framing instead of mouse-only inspection.

builder_motion.description: Timeline, authored clip, and scrub commands for builder-side motion work. These commands are easy to misunderstand if you assume staged timeline edits imply live playback.

builder_workbench.description: Builder subject, scaffold, selection, and workbench editing commands for embodiment work in the character forge.

capture_observer.description: Observer capture commands used to corroborate live scene, motion, and embodiment truth instead of trusting the viewport alone.

character_runtime.description: Mounted runtime commands for imported asset inspection, inhabitant presence, animation state, and clip control.

focus_navigation.description: Focus, replay, and sample navigation controls that steer what the theater shell is currently centered on.

surface_bridge.description: Owned-surface controls for opening, focusing, inspecting, and sending input into browser-side panels.

theater_profile_recipe.description: Top-level shell controls for theater mode, camera mode, replay mode, profiles, and recipes.

environment_misc.description: Cross-cutting environment controls that affect shell state, profile kits, world profile, or scene filters.

camera_frame_focus.summary: Frame the camera around the current focus target before capture or close inspection.
when_to_use: Use this when focus changed and the camera did not leave you in a useful composition.
Use it before capture_probe or capture_supercam when you want a clean baseline frame.

focus_actor.summary: Move theater focus to the current actor target and let the shell rebuild around that actor context.

character_mount.summary: Mount the runtime character surface so the theater has an active character subject to inspect or animate.
gotchas: Mounting creates the active runtime subject; many character commands are meaningless before this.
Builder mode and mounted runtime are related but not identical surfaces.

character_get_animation_state.summary: Read the mounted runtime animation state for the active subject instead of guessing from the viewport.

workbench_set_timeline_cursor.title: Set Builder Timeline Cursor
workbench_set_timeline_cursor.summary: Scrub the active builder timeline to a given normalized/time/index position and apply that pose to the workbench mesh.

workbench_preview_settle.title: Preview Builder Settle
workbench_preview_settle.summary: Generate a short visible settle reaction timeline from current balance/load truth and stage it for scrubbed preview.
when_to_use: Use this after a destabilizing pose edit or timeline change when you want the system to propose a corrective reaction.
This is the first builder-side balance controller surface: preview first, then decide whether to keep the generated motion.
mode_notes: Preview settle forces pose mode when needed because the corrective reaction is emitted through batch pose transforms.
The generated settle is staged as a transient timeline and does not autoplay by itself.
When you call this through env_control, pass the settle spec as a JSON string in target_id. The browser command surface parses target_id into the settle spec.
gotchas: Preview settle does not mutate authored clips or permanently rewrite the timeline until you commit it.
If the current diagnostics do not show a recoverable balance reaction, preview generation can legitimately return nothing.
Do not trust the first mirrored shared_state read right after dispatch. The ingress/live-sync loop can lag a few seconds even when preview generation succeeded.

Verify the result from workbench_surface.settle_preview, workbench_surface.pose_transform_count, and corroboration.last_action after a short wait instead of assuming an immediate mirror miss means failure.
failure_modes: Current balance diagnostics did not produce a recoverable settle reaction.

workbench_commit_settle.title: Commit Builder Settle
workbench_commit_settle.summary: Accept the staged settle preview and merge it into the builder timeline or compile it as a clip.
when_to_use: Use this only after inspecting a generated settle preview that you want to keep.
Set target to clip when the corrective reaction should become a reusable authored clip instead of just extending the working timeline.
mode_notes: Commit settle depends on an active preview; it does not regenerate a new settle plan by itself.
Commit can force pose mode before applying the merged settle result.
gotchas: No-op unless a settle preview is already active.
Clip compilation happens only when target is clip; timeline is the default destination.
failure_modes: No active settle preview exists to merge or compile.
Merged settle timeline or authored clip compile failed.

workbench_assert_balance.title: Assert Builder Balance
workbench_assert_balance.summary: Evaluate the current builder balance/load/contact truth and record a stale-aware pass, warn, or fail gate result in mirrored workbench state.
when_to_use: Use this before committing a pose, compiling a clip, or trusting a staged stance when you need a deterministic gate instead of eyeballing balance.
This is the workbench-side logic gate for downstream agent loops: assert first, then preview or commit settle when the stance fails.
mode_notes: This command evaluates current diagnostics only; it does not generate corrective motion.
Pass a JSON target_id payload to tune thresholds, for example {"max_risk":0.25,"allow_braced_support":true}.
The stored assertion becomes stale automatically when balance truth changes after the check.
gotchas: A previous PASS can become stale after later pose edits, cursor scrubs, motion preset changes, or settle operations.
The default gate is intentionally strict enough to catch obvious airborne, outside-polygon, penetration, and high-risk states.
failure_modes: No current balance/load diagnostics are available to evaluate.

character_set_model.title: Set Mounted Character Model
character_set_model.summary: Swap the mounted runtime to a direct asset ref and leave builder-subject mode for mesh-asset inspection.
when_to_use: Use this when you need to inspect an imported rigged asset instead of the builder subject.
For ground-interaction checks, prefer a footed character asset such as /static/assets/npc.glb or /static/assets/packs/kenney-blocky-characters/character-a.glb.
gotchas: Uses direct asset refs/URLs, not manifest aliases.
Switching model while builder is active deactivates the builder subject.
Do not use non-footed novelty rigs like GhostArmature as proof of planted feet or grounded motion.

workbench_new_builder.title: Create Builder Subject
workbench_new_builder.summary: Instantiate the preset builder subject, clear the mounted asset clone, and enter builder-subject workbench mode.

target_contract.description: Builder family id or JSON payload specifying family, mode, and anchor.

workbench_set_display_scope.title: Set Workbench Display Scope
workbench_set_display_scope.summary: Change whether the workbench shows the whole builder subject or a scoped part/adjacent/chain view for the current selected bone.

target_contract.description: Builder display scope enum for the currently selected part.

when_to_use: Use this when you want the browser and text theater to stage the selected part or chain as the current work cell instead of the whole body.
This is the main scope switch for proc-gen body-part work, isolated chain review, and scoped framing parity checks.
mode_notes: This depends on a selected builder bone; without selection, body scope is the only meaningful state.
Scope changes should be validated against both the web workbench guide and the paired text theater snapshot when parity matters.
gotchas: Turntable/manual camera behavior is separate from scope; scope changes alter the staged visible set and part framing inputs.
If the attached text_theater payload reports stale freshness, corroborate with env_read(query='text_theater_snapshot') before trusting the scoped frame as final.

workbench_set_pose.title: Set Builder Pose
workbench_set_pose.summary: Apply a single explicit pose transform to one builder bone using the pose gizmo contract instead of guessing through the viewport.

target_contract.description: Single-bone pose payload. Accepts bone_id/bone/canonical_joint plus either rotation quaternion or rotation_deg/rotation_degrees Euler triples. Offsets are honored only on hips/root-style bones.

when_to_use: Use this for isolated bone edits when you want one controlled nudge before reading the resulting balance/load shift.
Use batch only after you know the single-bone direction you want; this command is the simplest way to probe a specific joint.
what_it_changes: One builder pose transform in the active pose store.
Workbench selection/focus moves to the edited bone so the gizmo and overlays follow the edit.
mode_notes: Pose mode is required. Structure mode will reject the edit instead of silently staging it.
rotation_deg and rotation_degrees are valid shorthand for Euler degrees; raw rotation quaternion payloads still work too.
When you call this through env_control, pass the whole JSON pose payload as a string in target_id. The browser command surface reads target_id and normalizes it into the pose spec.
gotchas: Non-root offsets are intentionally rejected to preserve scaffold continuity. If you need translational repositioning, use hips/root-aware offsets only.
A torso lean alone will often stay inside a broad two-foot support base, so settle may still classify the state as ankle/mild.

Immediate env_read(query='shared_state') snapshots can lie right after dispatch because the ingress/live-sync mirror has not caught up yet. Give the browser a short beat, then verify pose_transform_count, posed_bone_ids, and corroboration.last_action.
failure_modes: Bone id is missing or not part of the current builder skeleton.
Mounted runtime mesh/workbench anchor is unavailable.

workbench_set_pose_batch.title: Set Builder Pose Batch
workbench_set_pose_batch.summary: Apply multiple coordinated pose edits atomically across the builder skeleton so lean, leg placement, and counterbalance can move together in one operation.

target_contract.description: Batch pose payload with a poses array. Each entry accepts bone_id/bone/canonical_joint plus rotation quaternion or rotation_deg/rotation_degrees Euler triples. Offsets are still hips/root-only even inside the batch.

when_to_use: Use this when the body must move as a coupled system: legs, feet, torso, and arms in one atomic edit.
This is the right builder surface for destabilizing or rebalancing a stance before calling workbench_preview_settle.
what_it_changes: Multiple builder pose transforms in one commit so the scaffold, gizmo state, and diagnostics update from the combined pose instead of partial intermediate steps.
The resulting motion diagnostics and load-field readings for the posed body.
mode_notes: Pose mode is required. The batch command will reject the entire request if the workbench is still in structure mode.
Canonical joint names are accepted, so payloads can use the embodiment-level ids instead of only raw bone ids.
rotation_deg / rotation_degrees are the cleanest payload for tool-driven edits; you do not need to hand-build quaternions.
When you call this through env_control, pass the entire JSON batch payload as a string in target_id. The normalized batch spec is built from target_id.
gotchas: Broad planted feet can keep the support polygon large even when the torso leans hard, so settle may still resolve to ankle/mild unless the support base is actually narrowed or skewed.
Offsets on non-hips bones are rejected even inside the batch. This is rotational pose editing, not arbitrary limb dragging.
If you want a real recover/step reaction, drive the legs and feet toward an asymmetric stance instead of only bending the chest.
The array key is poses, not bones. Older guesses like {"bones":[...]} normalize to an empty batch and make the command look inert.
Immediate shared_state reads can make a valid batch look dead. Wait a few seconds, then verify workbench_surface.pose_transform_count, workbench_surface.posed_bone_ids, and corroboration.last_action.
failure_modes: Mounted runtime mesh/workbench anchor is unavailable.

workbench_get_blueprint.title: Get Builder Blueprint
workbench_get_blueprint.summary: Return the serialized builder blueprint so the workbench state can be inspected as data instead of inferred visually.

capture_probe.title: Capture Probe
capture_probe.summary: Capture the multi-angle local probe view for the current subject or focused object.
when_to_use: Use this for close corroboration after timeline scrub, model swap, scaffold toggle, or load-field checks.
For mounted runtime validation, an explicit target hint like character_runtime::mounted_primary is the most reliable path.
mode_notes: In the current theater lane this is an env_control command, not a standalone /api/tool/capture_probe endpoint.
Treat the saved probe_*.jpg as the proof artifact; mirror fields help but the file is the durable corroboration.
gotchas: This is dispatched through the environment control lane; /api/tool/capture_probe is not a standalone tool in this build.
A blank target can still work when focus is stable, but explicit target ids are safer when the shell may have drifted.

capture_supercam.title: Capture Supercam
capture_supercam.summary: Capture the broader overview observer montage for scene-scale corroboration.

capture_time_strip.title: Capture Time Strip
capture_time_strip.summary: Capture sampled frames across the current builder timeline to inspect staged motion without live playback.
when_to_use: Use this after timeline scrub, settle preview, or authored-clip staging when you need evidence across the current builder timeline instead of a single scrubbed frame.
Use this for grounded motion validation when the timeline is staged but not running as live playback.
mode_notes: In the current theater lane this is an env_control command, not a standalone /api/tool/capture_time_strip endpoint.
The saved time_strip_*.jpg is the primary artifact. latest_time_strip_motion_summary in shared_state can lag behind the written file.
gotchas: This is dispatched through the environment control lane; /api/tool/capture_time_strip is not a standalone tool in this build.
The JPEG can be written before latest_time_strip_motion_summary settles in shared_state, so corroborate with the saved file when mirror fields lag.

open_surface.title: Open Surface
open_surface.summary: Open an owned browser surface inside the theater shell so it can be focused, inspected, or driven through bridge inputs.

surface_input.title: Surface Input
surface_input.summary: Send text or value input to an owned browser surface without relying on manual typing in the live browser.
gotchas: Surface input needs a valid surface id and target selector or control mapping.
Surface bridge success is easiest to confirm from visible browser behavior plus feed/shared_state.

workbench_set_load_field.title: Set Load Field
workbench_set_load_field.summary: Toggle or set the builder load-field mechanic lane used by scaffold-side diagnostics.
gotchas: This is the persistent runtime control, not the browser-local helper chip action.
Load mechanics can be active even when the visible load substrate is still intentionally minimal or deferred.

workbench_set_scaffold.title: Set Scaffold
workbench_set_scaffold.summary: Toggle scaffold presentation for the builder subject.

text_theater_snapshot.title: Text Theater Snapshot
text_theater_snapshot.summary: Read the canonical text-theater snapshot built in the live browser from theater, runtime, workbench, embodiment, balance, and corroboration state.
when_to_use: Use this when you need the full machine-readable text-theater contract rather than a rendered paragraph or capture artifact.
This is the right surface for agents, debugging tools, and future popup/text renderers because it carries the full namespaced snapshot.
mode_notes: The browser builds this snapshot from live runtime state and the server only passes it through.
The snapshot includes whole-theater context, not just bones or balance fields, so later renderers can stay paired to the same truth.
gotchas: Freshness fields matter. If the browser has not synced recently, stale_flags will tell you the read may reflect older live state.
Use this when you need structure. If you only need a quick human-readable readout, text_theater or text_theater_embodiment is usually cheaper.

text_theater.title: Text Theater Render
text_theater.summary: Read the paired text render derived from the canonical text-theater snapshot, with the live Scene plus Quarter/Profile companion views and camera/status footer.
when_to_use: Use this instead of capture_probe when you need a quick theater-wide read without waiting for image artifacts.
This is the fast paired operator surface when you want the live scene, two orbit-style companion views, and camera/runtime context in one terminal read.
Use this when you want a direct read on demand; theater-affecting env_control results now attach paired text_theater observation automatically.
gotchas: This is a rendered summary, not the full contract. Use text_theater_snapshot for full machine-readable state.
The output is truthful to the current live browser state, so freshness still depends on sync health.
Quarter/Profile are purpose-built text companion views, not raw topdown copies of the web capture tiles.
If a recent env_control result already attached text_theater.current_full/current_compact, prefer that payload first; it was captured for the command you just ran.

text_theater_embodiment.title: Text Theater Embodiment
text_theater_embodiment.summary: Read the embodiment/workbench text render showing bone tree, workbench selection, gizmo state, contacts, balance, settle, and timeline.
when_to_use: Use this instead of repeated captures when you need fast spatial reasoning about builder pose, workbench state, and settle truth.
This is the most useful Phase 1 text-theater readout during pose editing, batch pose work, and settle preview tuning.
Use it together with the render view when you want the visible paired body plus the structural bone/workbench state beside it.
gotchas: The bone tree is only one part of the output. The render also depends on live workbench, contact, balance, and settle surfaces.
If the browser has not synced recently, freshness fields in text_theater_snapshot will tell you whether the paired read is lagging.

text_theater_view.title: Text Theater View
text_theater_view.summary: Render the on-demand text-theater frame from the current mirrored shared_state, with consult/render/split modes and explicit section selection.
when_to_use: Use this when you need the actual current text-theater frame on demand instead of only the baked text strings or raw snapshot contract.
This is the direct manual read surface behind the command-attached observation payloads returned by theater-affecting env_control calls.
mode_notes: view=split is the most complete operator frame because it carries Theater and Embodiment together.
view=consult is the compact orientation surface used for lighter attached reads.
Observation queries do not mutate runtime state by themselves.
gotchas: Freshness still depends on mirror health. If the browser has not synced yet, the rendered frame can be truthful to older state.
When an env_control result already includes text_theater.current_full/current_compact plus freshness, prefer that payload first because it was captured in direct response to the command dispatch.
Use text_theater_snapshot when you need the full machine-readable contract instead of a rendered frame.

workbench-toggle-turntable.title: Toggle Workbench Turntable
workbench-toggle-turntable.summary: Flip the browser-local turntable for the current workbench subject. This is session-local and not callable through env_control.
gotchas: Turntable is not a backend env_control command.
This action changes browser session state directly.

workbench-toggle-load-field.title: Toggle Workbench Load Field
workbench-toggle-load-field.summary: Flip the helper-strip load toggle immediately in the browser and then dispatch workbench_set_load_field for persistence.

workbench-toggle-scaffold.title: Toggle Workbench Scaffold
workbench-toggle-scaffold.summary: Flip scaffold visibility from the helper strip and then dispatch workbench_set_scaffold so the runtime state follows.

workbench-set-locomotion.title: Set Workbench Locomotion Preview
workbench-set-locomotion.summary: Change the browser-side locomotion preview lane for the current workbench clip without pretending that it is a persistent builder command.
gotchas: Locomotion preview is browser-side preview state.
It can clear clip overrides in some cases when preview and clip state conflict.

workbench-toggle-pause.title: Toggle Workbench Pause
workbench-toggle-pause.summary: Pause or resume the local preview action object in the workbench clip strip.
gotchas: This is local preview control, not the same thing as staged builder timeline scrub.

reset-camera.title: Reset Camera UI Control
reset-camera.summary: Use the local cockpit control to forward camera reset through camera_reset_pose.

mounted_asset_inspection.title: Mounted Asset Inspection
notes: Use this when validating imported assets outside builder-subject mode.
For ground-interaction validation, use a footed rig with a readable silhouette. npc.glb is the current default. GhostArmature is good for mounted-path plumbing, not planted-feet truth.
Corroborate floor planting with captures, not viewport impression alone.

builder_helper_strip_review.title: Builder Helper Strip Review
notes: Use this when validating what is browser-local versus what persists through runtime commands.
Turntable is local-only; scaffold and load helper toggles bridge into persistent commands.

builder_blueprint_roundtrip.title: Builder Blueprint Roundtrip
notes: Use this when verifying builder state as serialized data instead of relying on the viewport.
Capture after reload so the rehydrated builder state is corroborated visually.

dual_theater_isolation_review.title: Dual Theater Isolation Review
notes: Use this when validating web/text parity for full body, isolated part, adjacent part, and chain staging.
The important corroboration fields are selected_part_surface, part_camera_recipes, workbench_stage_guide, and embodiment.scaffold_pieces visibility.
If body mode and text theater disagree on visible limb scaffold slots, treat that as a renderer/export regression and compare the browser-visible scaffold set against the text-rendered scaffold set.
Use the repo-local scripts/eval_isolation_scopes.py harness when you want repeatable browser-vs-text comparisons recorded as JSON.
